title: Molongum macrophyllum
intro: Molongum macrophyllum, also known as the giant mulberry or Bignay, is a tropical plant species native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. The health benefits of Molongum macrophyllum include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its traditional use in treating various health conditions, including fever and skin disorders. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Molongum macrophyllum is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tea, and it is also used to make herbal infusions and tinctures.
taxonomy: Molongum macrophyllum is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Menispermaceae. This plant is native to New Caledonia and is known for its large leaves and unique characteristics.
